author: agnxsh
content:
> This is why the EL needs to remember ILs for the tip of each branch.
<@845691100072509460> don't you think the ELs would spend a pretty decent chunk of mem caching ILs across different branches in a really forked network? i'm wondering if there's any protocol level changes that can be made to reduce these overheads.

author: jih2nn
content: The max size of all ILs is 128KiB. How many forks do we usually consider as a badly forked network?

author: tersec
content: The trouble is that a nonfinalizing network can in theory go back to very old forks, even if they're one block long

author: jih2nn
content: My point is how much the increased memory usage by ILs is meaningful when we're talking about 300M gas block.

author: tersec
content: in a finalizing network, negligible. In a nonfinalizing network, blocks still can't be larger than 10MB (CL gossip constraints), but it's not hard to start spending 15, 20+ MB on nothing but ILs, in a growing way
